steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage presents a paradox: CLPs are shielded during transmission (ingested by other hosts), yet their abundance declined as temperatures rose, while directly transmitted parasites—exposed during transmission—remained largely stable.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: D resolves the paradox: the protective transmission advantage of three-host CLPs “did not completely offset the negative effects of other temperature-driven factors” on their abundance. This explains why CLPs declined despite being shielded during transmission. The other choices misrepresent the protection mechanisms, contradict the findings, or introduce transmission-strategy distinctions the passage doesn't make. That matches choice D — D is correct.
